explanation: The property states that it is always the case that the system logs the results of the pre-use check. The keyword "shall" implies a global always (G) property. Therefore, template 7 `(G bool_exp3)` is the most suitable, where `bool_exp3` is replaced with `logPreUseCheck`.
The property states that it is globally true that the results of the pre-use check are logged. This captures the requirement that the system 'shall provide' this log, implying it must always do so.

The phrase "The system shall provide a log to include results of the pre-use check" indicates a global requirement. The LTL operator 'G' (Globally) is used to express that the atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck' must always be true. This captures the continuous and mandatory nature of the system providing the log for pre-use check results.

The property states that "logPreUseCheck" must always be true. This means the system continuously provides a log that includes the results of the pre-use check, ensuring this logging functionality is perpetually available and active as required by the statement "The system shall provide a log".

The property states that whenever the pre-use check is completed (bool_exp2), a log including its results (bool_exp3) will eventually be provided. This captures the requirement for logging pre-use check results as a persistent response to the completion of the check.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the pre-use check is indicated as done (preUseCheckDone), the system must, in the very next state (X), log the results of the pre-use check (logPreUseCheck). This ensures that every completion of a pre-use check is immediately followed by its results being logged.
The property states that 'logPreUseCheck' must always be true. This means the system must continuously provide a log of the pre-use check results.

The property states that the system will always (Globally) log the results of the pre-use check. This aligns with the requirement "The system shall provide a log to include results of the pre-use check", implying a continuous or ever-present functionality of logging these results.

The property states that it is always true that the system provides a log including the results of the pre-use check. This is captured by the 'Globally' operator (G) applied to the atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck', indicating a continuous requirement for the log to be available and contain the specified results.

This property states that globally, whenever the pre-use check is done (bool_exp2), then eventually, the system will log the results of the pre-use check (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement for the system to ensure logging of pre-use check results as a response to the completion of the check.
